title: Ridden Down
content:
When I taught Ida how to ride a
    Bicycle that night,
    I ran beside her, just to guide her
    Erring wheel aright;
    And many times there in the street
    She rode upon my weary feet.

    But now can Ida mount and ride a
    Wheel with graceful ease,
    And I, untiring in admiring,
    Fall upon my knees
    To worship her, and, for her part,
    She rides upon my proffered heart!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ellis Parker Butler, titled "Ridden Down", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
